summary.intent: Implement Anthropic prompt caching for Moltis by adding cache_control breakpoints to system prompts and last user messages, then extend it with a configurable CacheRetention enum matching OpenClaw's feature set.
summary.outcome: Full prompt caching implementation shipped: Anthropic direct + OpenRouter Anthropic models get cache_control blocks, CacheRetention config enum (none/short/long) added with default 'short', cache diagnostics logging added, 404 tests passing, PR #495 created and review comment fixed.

repo: Anthropic prompt caching requires cache_control blocks on system (as content-block array) and last user message — the beta header 'prompt-caching-2024-07-31' is no longer needed as caching is now GA
OpenRouter passes cache_control through to Anthropic when model ID starts with 'anthropic/' — detected via base URL containing 'openrouter.ai'
All other providers (OpenAI, DeepSeek, Ollama, vLLM, local GGUF) cache automatically with no client action needed
Provider config flows through ProviderEntry in crates/config/src/schema.rs → registered in crates/providers/src/lib.rs → passed into provider structs via builder pattern
AnthropicProvider uses a builder pattern with with_reasoning_effort() and now with_cache_retention() — cloned fields must be propagated through all builder methods
OpenClaw's pi-ai library only adds cache_control to the last user/assistant message at OpenRouter level; system-level caching is handled by a separate wrapper — Moltis handles both in one place
The genai crate wraps Gemini/other providers but doesn't expose Gemini's cached content API (which requires a separate two-step mechanism)

code.finding: to_anthropic_messages() signature changed from returning Option<String> to Option<Vec<Value>> for system, now accepts caching: bool parameter to conditionally inject cache_control blocks
complete() passes caching_enabled() to to_anthropic_messages and sets body["system"] as array
stream_with_tools() mirrors complete() for cache_control injection
apply_openrouter_cache_control() injects cache_control on system message and last user message, skips when cache_retention is None
CacheRetention enum (None/Short/Long) added to ProviderEntry with serde skip-if-default, default is Short
CacheRetention must be explicitly re-exported from the config crate lib.rs
register_builtin_providers reads cache_retention from ProviderEntry config and passes to both AnthropicProvider and OpenAiProvider via with_cache_retention()

workflow: Deep-diving the upstream reference implementation (OpenClaw/pi-ai) directly on disk revealed that the Anthropic beta header was unnecessary — saved a stale header being sent in production
Running cargo fmt after every edit batch prevents formatting drift from accumulating
PR review comments should be resolved via gh CLI by resolving the conversation thread after pushing the fix commit

friction: Multiple rounds of reading openai.rs were needed to understand the full structure before finding the right insertion points for apply_openrouter_cache_control
The with_reasoning_effort() builder method in both AnthropicProvider and OpenAiProvider required updating to carry the new cache_retention field — easy to miss
Xcode build failure due to pre-existing IDESimulatorFoundation/DVTDownloads symbol mismatch in the CI/build environment (unrelated to changes)
Indentation misalignment in debug! macro (12 spaces vs 16) required a post-PR-creation fix — rustfmt didn't catch it
open_items: Bedrock Anthropic caching — no Bedrock provider exists yet; when added it should support cache_control the same way as direct Anthropic
Gemini context caching — requires a separate two-step API (pre-create CachedContent resource, then reference by ID); fundamentally different design, deferred
CacheRetention 'long' variant — currently parsed and stored but behaves identically to 'short' (both use ephemeral); long TTL hint support requires provider-level differentiation
Cache diagnostics JSONL tracing (like OpenClaw's diagnostics.cacheTrace) — deferred as nice-to-have
Heartbeat keep-warm to prevent cache eviction — deferred as advanced optimization
Context pruning in cache-ttl mode (prune stale tool results after cache TTL) — separate feature, out of scope for this PR
